what_it_does: Defines the reduce_synth role: a generate-role that takes a composed notes input and merges N map-output notes into a single summary, exposing a Pydantic output schema and runtime metadata.
observations: This file is almost entirely a single ROLE dictionary plus the ReduceSynthOut Pydantic model.
The role's op is generate (default) and its only declared input axis is notes.
model_binding declares required capabilities [chat, json, reasoning] but sets default_model and default_base_url to None.
A single rules entry of kind reduce describes merging N map-output notes into one summary.
render_hint specifies shape summary and lane reduce.
The docstring and comments repeatedly note the role is not in any mode_scope and is fired explicitly by run_reduce(mode='role').
The prompt template asks for JSON with one field summary and gives an example.
standouts: Capability-based model binding rather than a hardcoded model name.
Dynamic N map outputs are composed by the driver (run_reduce) and supplied as a single notes input, not statically addressed.
The reduce rule is declared as data while the actual join model call happens inside the role.
conventions: Uses pydantic BaseModel for output schema validation.
Role definition is a flat dictionary with id, label, description, prompt_template, output_schema, input_addresses, op, trigger, model_binding, rules, render_hint.
input_addresses is a tuple of non-utterance input labels.
rules entries include id, reads, effect, and kind.
Extensive inline documentation explains runtime semantics and scope.
concerns: default_model and default_base_url are None, so correct runtime routing depends entirely on resolve_model.
The prompt template requests JSON but does not inject the schema verbatim, relying on the model to follow instructions.
The rule reads field reduce_synth.notes, which is self-referential and its resolution semantics are not defined in this file.
No mode_scope means it will not be triggered by the listening cast unless explicitly invoked.
notes: A declarative, capability-annotated reduce-role join that expects the runtime to compose upstream notes and route to a reasoning-capable model.
questions: How exactly does run_reduce read the N dynamic run:// addresses and format them into the notes input?
What does resolve_model do when default_model is None and no resident model matches the required capabilities?
Are shape and lane in render_hint purely UI metadata or do they affect scheduling or lane assignment?
What happens if the notes input is missing or empty?
Is the [unit id] prefix in each note line guaranteed by the driver or is it the role's responsibility to parse?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it declares a reduce-role that merges N map outputs into one summary.
apparent_themes: Separation of runtime driver logic from role logic
intention_signals: To make the abstract reduce-tree join role concrete as a runtime-callable, capability-aware generate-role while keeping input composition and model selection outside the file.
novelty: It treats a reduce join as a generate role with a dynamically composed notes input and a capability-requirement model binding, rather than hardcoding upstream addresses or a specific model.
contribution: A reusable reduce-role template and schema that shows how cross-unit synthesis can be declared as data, leaving dynamic input composition and reasoning-capable model resolution to the runtime.
summary_for_embedding: roles/reduce_synth.py defines the reduce_synth generate-role with a Pydantic summary output schema, a notes input axis, capability-based model binding, and a reduce rule for merging N map-output notes into one summary.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a declarative, runtime-fired reduce-role join that synthesizes composed map outputs into a single summary while remaining outside the automatic listening cast.

symbols.does: Pydantic model that defines the reduce-role output as a single summary string.
symbols.description: ReduceSynthOut is the output schema for the reduce_synth role. It expects a JSON object with one field, summary, which holds the merged result across the composed upstream notes. By inheriting from BaseModel, it gives the runtime a validated, parseable target for the generate op.
